name: Dog Food
whatToLookFor: AAFCO statement confirming complete & balanced nutrition
Named animal protein as the first ingredient
Formula matched to life stage (puppy/adult/senior)
Vet guidance for breed-specific or medical needs
overview: Choosing dog food comes down to matching AAFCO-certified nutrition to your dog's life stage, size, and any health conditions. There is no single 'best' brand for every dog -- a vet or veterinary nutritionist can help with dogs that have specific medical needs.

name: Cat Food
whatToLookFor: AAFCO statement for complete & balanced nutrition
High animal protein content (cats are obligate carnivores)
Wet vs dry balance for hydration
Life-stage-appropriate formula
overview: Cats are obligate carnivores with higher protein needs than dogs. Many vets recommend at least some wet food in the diet to support hydration, particularly for cats prone to urinary issues.

name: Flea & Tick Prevention
whatToLookFor: EPA or FDA approval status
Species-specific formulation (never use dog product on a cat)
Local flea/tick season length
Vet recommendation for your pet's health history
overview: Flea and tick prevention products vary by species, weight, and region. It's important to use only products labeled for your specific pet -- some dog flea treatments are toxic to cats.

name: Leashes & Collars
whatToLookFor: Secure, well-stitched hardware
Correct collar fit (two fingers should fit under it)
Harness option for breeds prone to tracheal issues
Reflective material for visibility
overview: For breeds prone to tracheal collapse or neck sensitivity (small toy breeds), a harness is generally recommended over a collar for daily walks.

name: Orthopedic Dog Beds
whatToLookFor: Supportive foam density appropriate to weight
Washable cover
Size that lets the dog fully stretch out
Non-slip base
overview: Orthopedic beds are especially worth considering for senior dogs or breeds prone to joint conditions like hip dysplasia, since supportive foam reduces pressure on joints.

name: Cat Litter
whatToLookFor: Clumping vs non-clumping based on cleaning preference
Dust-free formulas for respiratory-sensitive cats
Unscented options (many cats dislike heavy fragrance)
Litter box size relative to cat size
overview: Cats can be particular about litter texture and scent, and sudden changes are a common cause of litter box avoidance -- transitioning gradually is generally recommended.

name: Joint Supplements
whatToLookFor: Glucosamine/chondroitin content
Vet recommendation, especially for diagnosed joint conditions
Species-appropriate dosing
Third-party testing where available
overview: Joint supplements are commonly recommended alongside -- not instead of -- veterinary treatment for conditions like hip or elbow dysplasia. Discuss dosing with a vet, particularly for growing puppies.

name: Dental Chews
whatToLookFor: VOHC (Veterinary Oral Health Council) seal of acceptance
Appropriate size/hardness for the dog's chewing style
Calorie content if the dog is weight-managed
overview: Dental chews can help reduce plaque buildup but don't replace brushing or professional cleanings, especially for small breeds prone to dental disease.
